answer: January 10, 2026 (corrected from initially recorded January 8)
January 10, 2027 — 12 months from the corrected issuance date
Yes — foundation inspection was requested March 5 and passed March 10, well within the July 10, 2026 commencement deadline (6 months from issuance)
A stop-work order was issued after the framing inspection failed on April 28 (load-bearing wall header undersized). Per §5.3, the permit was suspended.
39 calendar days (April 28 to June 6, using the corrected reinstatement date). Initially calculated as 38 days using the uncorrected June 5 date.
February 18, 2027. The permit was 108 days old at suspension. 365 - 108 = 257 remaining days. June 6 + 257 = February 18, 2027.
June 27, 2026 — 15 business days from the June 6 reinstatement
Yes — requested June 20, which is 10 business days after reinstatement, within the 15-business-day deadline
No — the MEP inspection was requested June 20 and completed July 2 (corrected from June 30). That is 8 business days, exceeding the 5-business-day requirement by 3 business days.
3 business days (8 actual minus 5 required). This is based on the corrected completion date of July 2; the uncorrected date of June 30 would have shown only 1 extra day.
A 3-business-day extension at no cost, per §5.5, because the Inspector exceeded the 5-business-day requirement by 3 days. Based on the corrected completion date.
February 21, 2027 — the adjusted expiration of February 18 plus 3 business days (accounting for a weekend)
PASSED — completed September 18, 2026
Yes — issued September 18, 2026 upon passing the final inspection
SUSPENDED — the suspension began April 28 and was not lifted until June 6. May 1 falls within the suspension period.
ACTIVE (reinstated) — the suspension was lifted June 6. June 10 is after reinstatement. The permit is active with a tolled expiration.
ACTIVE with Certificate of Occupancy issued — the CO was issued September 18. The permit is effectively complete.

The permit would have expired automatically. §5.1 requires construction to commence within 6 months of issuance (by July 10). August 1 is past the deadline. The permit would expire without construction having commenced.
Correction 2 — the reinstatement date changed from June 5 to June 6, which changed the suspension from 38 to 39 days and the adjusted expiration from February 19 to February 18.

Yes — Correction 3 changed the MEP completion from June 30 to July 2. Under June 30, the overage was 1 business day (6 minus 5), entitling 1 day of extension. Under July 2, the overage is 3 business days (8 minus 5), entitling 3 days. The correction tripled the extension entitlement.
January 10, 2026 — the township database initially had January 8, but the signed permit document shows January 10
A scheduling conflict with an emergency project on Birch Lane, per Inspector Ito's notes (in Italian)
No — the Inspector's note is an explanation and apology, not a finding of fault. She confirms the applicant's §5.5 extension entitlement is legitimate.
No — the Certificate of Occupancy was not issued until September 18. §5.6 states no occupancy is permitted before the CO is issued. September 17 is before the CO.
Foundation, framing, MEP (mechanical/electrical/plumbing), and final — in that order per §5.5
